title: Bucket Types, Endpoints, Regions, and S3 Express · Go Build Room
summary: A delivery team incrementally builds bucket-type and endpoint compatibility matrix, changes one file or failure, and keeps earlier HTTP, Go, accessibility, security, and resource-budget invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bucket-type and endpoint compatibility matrix while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare general-purpose and directory buckets by namespace, endpoint, zone, ordering, throughput, durability scope, versioning, lifecycle, and feature support.
Construct regional virtual-hosted and zonal endpoints without confusing region, availability zone, bucket name, TLS name, or path style.
Resolve intended region and endpoint explicitly and reject redirects, signing-region drift, and accidental cross-region transfer.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop General Directory Bucket
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 6775 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate general or directory bucket region, zone, endpoint, reactivation, and session rules; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare general-purpose and directory buckets by namespace, endpoint, zone, ordering, throughput, durability scope, versioning, lifecycle, and feature support.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop General Directory Bucket.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare general-purpose and directory buckets by namespace, endpoint, zone, ordering, throughput, durability scope, versioning, lifecycle, and feature support. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop General Directory Bucket, keep General Directory Bucket connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare general-purpose and directory buckets by namespace, endpoint, zone, ordering, throughput, durability scope, versioning, lifecycle, and feature support.
